name: sign-up.tsx: 7 Button labels hardcoded in English
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L12-01] all 7 sign-up button labels (Try Again, Continue with Google/Apple/OpenAI, Sign up, Sign in...) are hardcoded; Button forwards label to text AND accessibilityLabel. sign-in localizes the equivalents. Fix: add auth.signUp.* keys to all 7 locales and route through t().
acceptanceCriteria:
Root cause: sign-up.tsx at lines 565, 574, 589, 602, 615, 677, and 694 passes hardcoded English string literals as label props to Button components ("Try Again", "Try another method", "Continue with Google", "Continue with Apple", "Continue with OpenAI", "Sign up", "Sign in"). The Button component forwards the label to both the visible Text and accessibilityLabel. sign-in.tsx localizes equivalent buttons through t(). None of the seven auth.signUp.* keys for these labels exist in en.json.

Variants (seven, all in sign-up.tsx): (a) Try Again (:565), (b) Try another method (:574), (c) Continue with Google (:589), (d) Continue with Apple (:602), (e) Continue with OpenAI (:615), (f) Sign up (:677), (g) Sign in (:694). Note: "Try Again" can reuse existing common.tryAgain; the other six need new auth.signUp.* keys.

Fix: (1) add keys auth.signUp.tryAnotherMethod, auth.signUp.continueWithGoogle, auth.signUp.continueWithApple, auth.signUp.continueWithOpenAI, auth.signUp.signUpButton, auth.signUp.signInLink to en.json and all 6 other locale files via pnpm translate; (2) replace the 7 hardcoded label values: label={t('common.tryAgain')} for :565, and the respective new auth.signUp.* keys for the remaining six.

Verification: run pnpm check:i18n:orphans — all 6 new keys must appear as referenced. On a non-English locale, all 7 button labels must render in the target language.
riskImpact: The first screen new users see renders English buttons in all 6 non-English locales.
